# Rainy Days in the Kiso

> A slow, crowd-free rainy-day activity in the Kiso Valley (Nagiso, Nagano, Japan).
> Run out of Kashiwaya guesthouse by From Scratch LLC. Sister project to Beyond Nakasendo Cycling.

Rainy Days in the Kiso helps travellers enjoy a wet day in the Kiso Valley instead of waiting it out.
The site is written for visitors who come in Japan's quiet off-season and prefer slow, uncrowded travel —
typically families and couples from Europe, North America and Australia.

## The flagship activity

- **The Nerikiri Challenge**: a hands-on workshop, about 2 hours, held indoors by an irori hearth inside a
  century-old kura storehouse. Guests shape nerikiri — delicate seasonal Japanese sweets (sweetened
  white-bean paste, hand-shaped into flowers, leaves and fruit) — with a local guide, then eat their own
  creation with a bowl of matcha. All skill levels, family-friendly, matcha included.
- **Booking**: reserve about one month ahead. Nerikiri is made fresh in small batches and the bean paste is
  prepared by hand before guests arrive. Book via WhatsApp, email, or phone.

## Bonus / cross-sell

- **Shower Cycling**: for travellers who would rather ride through the weather than shelter from it —
  fat-tyre e-bikes through the valley's gorges in rain or snow. Run by the sister project, Beyond Nakasendo
  Cycling: https://kiso-ebike-lp.vercel.app/

## Contact

- Location: Nagiso, Kiso Valley, Nagano, Japan
- WhatsApp / phone: +81 90-3839-2354
